name: Influenza
description: Acute viral respiratory infection caused by influenza A or B virus, typically presenting with abrupt onset of fever, myalgia, headache, and dry cough.

name: Community-acquired pneumonia
description: Lower respiratory tract infection acquired outside the hospital, commonly bacterial (S. pneumoniae most frequent). Features productive cough, fever, dyspnea, focal chest signs.

name: Acute pharyngitis (Strep throat if GAS+)
description: Inflammation of the pharynx, most often viral; bacterial (Group A streptococcus) requires antibiotic therapy. Centor criteria help estimate GAS likelihood.
tests: Throat swab culture if RADT negative and high suspicion

name: Lower urinary tract infection
description: Infection of the bladder/urethra, predominantly E. coli in women. Presents with dysuria, urinary frequency, urgency, suprapubic pain.

name: Acute gastroenteritis
description: Inflammation of the GI tract, commonly viral (norovirus, rotavirus) or bacterial. Features acute diarrhea ± vomiting, abdominal cramps, often self-limiting.

name: Migraine without aura
description: Recurrent primary headache disorder; unilateral, pulsating headache lasting 4–72h, with nausea, photophobia and/or phonophobia.
tests: Neuroimaging only if red flags present

name: Essential hypertension
description: Persistently elevated arterial blood pressure ≥140/90 mmHg without identifiable secondary cause; major cardiovascular risk factor.

name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
description: Chronic metabolic disorder of insulin resistance and relative insulin deficiency leading to hyperglycemia.

name: Acute asthma exacerbation
description: Acute worsening of airway inflammation and bronchoconstriction; presents with wheeze, dyspnea, chest tightness, and reduced peak flow.

name: Acute coronary syndrome (suspected)
description: Spectrum including unstable angina and myocardial infarction. Central chest pain ± radiation, sweating, dyspnea — requires urgent evaluation.

name: Iron-deficiency anemia
description: Microcytic hypochromic anemia due to depleted iron stores; common causes include menstrual blood loss, GI loss, and dietary insufficiency.

name: Gastroesophageal reflux disease
description: Reflux of gastric contents causing heartburn, regurgitation, and esophageal mucosal injury.
tests: Upper GI endoscopy if alarm features

name: Acute bronchitis
description: Self-limiting lower-airway inflammation, almost always viral; cough lasting up to 3 weeks ± wheeze, chest discomfort. Antibiotics rarely indicated.
tests: Chest X-ray only if pneumonia suspected

name: Allergic rhinitis
description: IgE-mediated nasal mucosal inflammation triggered by aeroallergens; sneezing, rhinorrhea, nasal obstruction, itching.

name: Acute sinusitis
description: Symptomatic inflammation of paranasal sinus mucosa, usually viral but bacterial superinfection possible after >10 days.
tests: Sinus CT only if complication suspected

name: COPD exacerbation
description: Acute worsening of dyspnea, sputum volume, or sputum purulence in a patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

name: Pulmonary tuberculosis
description: Chronic granulomatous infection by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. Endemic in Egypt; presents with chronic cough, fever, weight loss, night sweats.
source: WHO Global TB 2023; Egyptian National TB Programme

name: Acute otitis media
description: Bacterial or viral infection of the middle ear; common in children. Otalgia, fever, bulging tympanic membrane.

name: Otitis externa
description: Inflammation of the external auditory canal; bacterial (P. aeruginosa, S. aureus) or fungal. Otalgia worsened by tragal pressure.
tests: Ear swab if recurrent or unresponsive

name: Peptic ulcer disease
description: Mucosal break in stomach or duodenum, mostly H. pylori or NSAID-related. Epigastric pain, dyspepsia, possible bleeding.
tests: H. pylori urea breath test or stool antigen
Upper GI endoscopy if alarm features

name: Helicobacter pylori infection
description: Chronic gastric infection associated with peptic ulcer disease, gastric MALT lymphoma, gastric cancer.
tests: Endoscopic biopsy with rapid urease test

name: Irritable bowel syndrome
description: Chronic functional bowel disorder of recurrent abdominal pain related to defecation, with altered stool form/frequency (Rome IV criteria).

name: Chronic functional constipation
description: Persistent difficult, infrequent, or incomplete defecation without organic cause.

name: Chronic hepatitis C
description: Chronic HCV infection — historically high prevalence in Egypt; now treatable with direct-acting antivirals via the national elimination program.
source: EASL 2020; Egyptian Ministry of Health 100M Healthy Lives

name: Acute hepatitis A
description: Acute self-limiting viral hepatitis transmitted fecal-orally; jaundice, anorexia, RUQ pain.

name: Biliary colic / cholelithiasis
description: Transient gallstone obstruction of the cystic duct; episodic RUQ pain after fatty meals.

name: Chronic heart failure
description: Chronic syndrome of impaired cardiac output (reduced or preserved EF) producing dyspnea, fatigue, edema.

name: Atrial fibrillation
description: Supraventricular tachyarrhythmia with irregularly irregular pulse; major embolic stroke risk requiring anticoagulation by CHA2DS2-VASc.

name: Stable angina
description: Predictable exertional chest discomfort relieved by rest or nitrates due to fixed coronary stenosis.

name: Dyslipidemia
description: Abnormal lipid profile — elevated LDL, low HDL, or hypertriglyceridemia — increasing atherosclerotic cardiovascular risk.

name: Deep vein thrombosis (suspected)
description: Thrombus in a deep vein of the lower limb; risk of pulmonary embolism. Unilateral leg swelling, pain, warmth.

name: Primary hypothyroidism
description: Reduced thyroid hormone production, mostly autoimmune (Hashimoto). Fatigue, cold intolerance, weight gain, bradycardia.

name: Hyperthyroidism (Graves disease)
description: Excess thyroid hormone, commonly autoimmune (Graves). Tachycardia, weight loss, heat intolerance, tremor, exophthalmos.

name: Diabetic ketoacidosis
description: Acute life-threatening metabolic decompensation in diabetes — hyperglycemia, ketosis, metabolic acidosis. Requires hospital management.

name: Obesity
description: Excess adiposity (BMI ≥30 kg/m²) increasing risk of T2DM, cardiovascular disease, and many cancers.

name: Tension-type headache
description: Bilateral pressing/tightening non-pulsating headache, mild–moderate, not aggravated by routine activity.

name: Epilepsy (generalized tonic-clonic)
description: Recurrent unprovoked seizures with loss of consciousness, tonic-clonic movements, postictal drowsiness.

name: Acute ischemic stroke (suspected)
description: Sudden focal neurological deficit from cerebral ischemia. Time-critical — needs urgent imaging and possible thrombolysis/thrombectomy.

name: Bell's palsy
description: Acute idiopathic unilateral lower motor neuron facial nerve palsy. Diagnosis of exclusion.

name: Benign paroxysmal positional vertigo
description: Brief positional vertigo from otoconia displacement in the semicircular canals. Dix-Hallpike positive.

name: Acute pyelonephritis
description: Bacterial infection of the renal parenchyma; flank pain, fever, rigors, often with lower UTI symptoms.

name: Renal colic / nephrolithiasis
description: Severe loin-to-groin colicky pain from ureteric stone obstruction.

name: Benign prostatic hyperplasia
description: Non-malignant prostatic enlargement causing lower urinary tract symptoms in older men.

name: Atopic dermatitis (eczema)
description: Chronic relapsing pruritic inflammatory skin disorder, often with personal/family atopy.

name: Acne vulgaris
description: Chronic inflammatory disorder of pilosebaceous units; comedones, papules, pustules, scarring.

name: Tinea corporis
description: Superficial dermatophyte infection of trunk/limb skin — annular scaly plaques with central clearing.
tests: Skin scraping for KOH microscopy and fungal culture

name: Scabies
description: Pruritic dermatosis caused by Sarcoptes scabiei mite. Burrows in interdigital webs, wrists, axillae, genitalia. Worse at night.

name: Urticaria
description: Transient pruritic wheals from mast-cell-mediated histamine release; acute (<6 wks) or chronic.

name: Bacterial conjunctivitis
description: Bacterial infection of the bulbar/palpebral conjunctiva — purulent discharge, eyelid sticking, conjunctival injection.
tests: Conjunctival swab if severe or unresponsive

name: Schistosomiasis (urinary/intestinal)
description: Parasitic infection by Schistosoma haematobium (urinary) or S. mansoni (intestinal). Endemic in parts of rural Egypt.

name: Typhoid fever
description: Systemic infection by Salmonella enterica serovar Typhi. Stepwise fever, relative bradycardia, abdominal pain, rose spots.

name: Amoebiasis (intestinal)
description: Infection by Entamoeba histolytica — bloody diarrhea, tenesmus, possible hepatic abscess.

name: Mechanical low back pain
description: Non-specific musculoskeletal low back pain without red flags; usually self-limiting.
tests: Imaging only if red flags (trauma, neurological deficit, malignancy, infection)

name: Osteoarthritis (knee)
description: Degenerative joint disease — joint pain worse with activity, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, reduced range of motion.

name: Acute gout
description: Monosodium urate crystal arthropathy — sudden severe monoarthritis, classically first MTP joint, with redness and swelling.

name: Rheumatoid arthritis
description: Chronic symmetrical inflammatory polyarthritis, predominantly small joints, with morning stiffness >1h. Autoimmune (RF, anti-CCP).

name: Major depressive disorder
description: Persistent low mood, anhedonia, and neurovegetative symptoms ≥2 weeks impairing function.

name: Generalized anxiety disorder
description: Excessive uncontrollable worry on most days for ≥6 months with somatic symptoms.

name: Chronic insomnia
description: Difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep ≥3 nights/week for ≥3 months with daytime impairment.

name: Vitamin B12 deficiency anemia
description: Megaloblastic anemia from B12 deficiency; macrocytosis, fatigue, glossitis, neuropathy.
